<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check frame 14 position after UP 5
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        child_colors = [nodes[c]['color'] for c in n['children']]
        print(f"N id={n['id']}: rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, children={child_colors}, hash={n['hash']}")

# Check ascii around the gap to see if frame can pass through
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nAscii rows 25-35, cols 18-36:")
for r in range(25, 36):
    if r < len(lines):
        print(f"  row {r}: {lines[r][18:36]}")
</parameter>

sections.content:
Now I can see:
- Frame 7 (hash b5d5514416984043, the 6x6 frame): rows 30-35, cols 30-35, with white center (selected)
- The gap in the purple barrier is at rows 27-29, cols 30+ (gray area)
- The frame is at rows 30-35, which is just below the gap
Looking at the ascii:
- Rows 27-29: purple on left (cols 18-29), gray on right (cols 30-35) - this is the gap
- Rows 30-35: white on left (cols 18-20), purple (cols 21-29), then the frame (cols 30-35)
The frame is 6 rows tall (30-35). The gap is at rows 27-29. If I move the frame UP by 3 (one UP), it would be at rows 27-32. The top 3 rows (27-29) would be in the gap area, and the bottom 3 rows (30-32) would be below.
But wait - looking at the ascii, rows 30-35 at cols 30-35 show the frame (NNNNNN). The gap at rows 27-29 at cols 30-35 is gray (empty). So the frame can move UP into the gap.
Let me try moving UP one more time to get the frame to rows 27-32, then LEFT to cross through the gap.
Actually, looking more carefully at the ascii:
- Row 27-29, cols 30-35: gggggg (gray, empty)
- Row 30-35, cols 30-35: NNNNNN (the frame)
So the frame is at rows 30-35. If I move UP once (3 rows), it goes to rows 27-32. The top 3 rows (27-29) would be in the gap. Then I can try to move LEFT.
But the frame is 6 rows tall. When at rows 27-32, rows 27-29 are in the gap (where cols 30+ are clear), but rows 30-32 have the purple bar at cols 21-29. Since the frame is at cols 30-35, it doesn't overlap with the purple bar at cols 21-29.
So the plan:
1. Move UP 1 more time to get frame to rows 27-32
2. Move LEFT to cross through the gap
Let me try UP, then LEFT.
